content.title: THE IMPORTANCE OF CHILDREN'S PLAY
content.notes: ['[1] Lego: coloured plastic building blocks and other pieces that can be joined together']

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Brick by brick, six-year-old Alice is building a magical kingdom. Imagining fairy-tale turrets and fire-breathing dragons, wicked witches and gallant heroes, she's creating an enchanting world. Although she isn't aware of it, this fantasy is helping her take her first steps towards her capacity for creativity and so it will have important repercussions in her adult life.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Minutes later, Alice has abandoned the kingdom in favour of playing schools with her younger brother. When she bosses him around as his 'teacher', she's practising how to regulate her emotions through pretence. Later on, when they tire of this and settle down with a board game, she's learning about the need to follow rules and take turns with a partner.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: 'Play in all its rich variety is one of the highest achievements of the human species,' says Dr David Whitebread from the Faculty of Education at the University of Cambridge, UK. 'It underpins how we develop as intellectual, problem-solving adults and is crucial to our success as a highly adaptable species.'

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Recognising the importance of play is not new: over two millennia ago, the Greek philosopher Plato extolled its virtues as a means of developing skills for adult life, and ideas about play-based learning have been developing since the 19th century.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: But we live in changing times, and Whitebread is mindful of a worldwide decline in play, pointing out that over half the people in the world now live in cities. 'The opportunities for free play, which I experienced almost every day of my childhood, are becoming increasingly scarce,' he says. Outdoor play is curtailed by perceptions of risk to do with traffic, as well as parents' increased wish to protect their children from being the victims of crime, and by the emphasis on 'earlier is better' which is leading to greater competition in academic learning and schools.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: International bodies like the United Nations and the European Union have begun to develop policies concerned with children's right to play, and to consider implications for leisure facilities and educational programmes. But what they often lack is the evidence to base policies on.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: 'The type of play we are interested in is child-initiated, spontaneous and unpredictable - but, as soon as you ask a five-year-old "to play", then you as the researcher have intervened,' explains Dr Sara Baker. And we want to know what the long-term impact of play is. It's a real challenge.'

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Dr Jenny Gibson agrees, pointing out that although some of the steps in the puzzle of how and why play is important have been looked at, there is very little data on the impact it has on the child's later life.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Now, thanks to the university's new Centre for Research on Play in Education, Development and Learning (PEDAL), Whitebread, Baker, Gibson and a team of researchers hope to provide evidence on the role played by play in how a child develops.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: 'A strong possibility is that play supports the early development of children's self-control,' explains Baker. 'This is our ability to develop awareness of our own thinking processes – it influences how effectively we go about undertaking challenging activities.'

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: In a study carried out by Baker with toddlers and young pre-schoolers, she found that children with greater self-control solved problems more quickly when exploring an unfamiliar set-up requiring scientific reasoning. 'This sort of evidence makes us think that giving children the chance to play will make them more successful problem-solvers in the long run.'

paragraphs.label: L
paragraphs.text: If playful experiences do facilitate this aspect of development, say the researchers, it could be extremely significant for educational practices, because the ability to self-regulate has been shown to be a key predictor of academic performance.

paragraphs.label: M
paragraphs.text: Gibson adds: 'Playful behaviour is also an important indicator of healthy social and emotional development. In my previous research, I investigated how observing children at play can give us important clues about their well-being and can even be useful in the diagnosis of neurodevelopmental disorders like autism.'

paragraphs.label: N
paragraphs.text: Whitebread's recent research has involved developing a play-based approach to supporting children's writing. 'Many primary school children find writing difficult, but we showed in a previous study that a playful stimulus was far more effective than an instructional one.' Children wrote longer and better-structured stories when they first played with dolls representing characters in the story. In the latest study, children first created their story with Lego [1], with similar results. 'Many teachers commented that they had always previously had children saying they didn't know what to write about. With the Lego building, however, not a single child said this through the whole year of the project.'

paragraphs.label: O
paragraphs.text: Whitebread, who directs PEDAL, trained as a primary school teacher in the early 1970s, when, as he describes, 'the teaching of young children was largely a quiet backwater, untroubled by any serious intellectual debate or controversy.' Now, the landscape is very different, with hotly debated topics such as school starting age.

paragraphs.label: P
paragraphs.text: 'Somehow the importance of play has been lost in recent decades. It's regarded as something trivial, or even as something negative that contrasts with "work". Let's not lose sight of its benefits, and the fundamental contributions it makes to human achievements in the arts, sciences and technology. Let's make sure children have a rich diet of play experiences.

groups.title: Children's play
groups.desc:
Questions 1-8

Complete the notes below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
groups.collect: Uses of children's play • building a 'magical kingdom' may help develop $635$ • board games involve $636$and turn-taking Recent changes affecting children's play • populations of $637$ have grown • opportunities for free play are limited due to     - fear of $638$     - fear of $639$     - increased $640$, in schools International policies on children's play • it is difficult to find $641$to support new policies • research needs to study the impact of play on the rest of the child's$642$

questions.analysis: 答案是 "creativity"。题干中的 "building a 'magical kingdom'" 对应文章中的 "this fantasy"，而 "may help develop" 对应文章中的 "is helping her take her first steps towards"。通过这些对应关系，可以确定答案所在的句子，并从中提取出 "creativity" 作为答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Although she isn't aware of it, this fantasy is helping her take her first steps towards her capacity for creativity and so it will have important repercussions in her adult life.
central_sentences.translation: 尽管她没有意识到，这种幻想正在帮助她迈出发展创造力能力的第一步，这将对她的成年生活产生重要影响。

target_sen.target_sen: Although she isn't aware of it, this fantasy is helping her take her first steps towards her capacity for creativity and so it will have important repercussions in her adult life.

syn_ary.synonym: take her first steps towards her capacity for

target_sen.target_sen: Although she isn't aware of it, this fantasy is helping her take her first steps towards her capacity for creativity and so it will have important repercussions in her adult life.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "board games involve" 对应文章中的 "settle down with a board game" 和 "learning about the need to"。题干中的 "turn-taking" 对应文章中的 "take turns"。在这句话中，"rules" 是唯一一个名词，直接对应题干的空格处。因此，正确答案是 "rules"。

central_sentences.sentence: Later on, when they tire of this and settle down with a board game, she's learning about the need to follow rules and take turns with a partner.
central_sentences.translation: 之后，当他们厌倦了这个游戏并开始玩棋盘游戏时，她正在学习遵守规则和与伙伴轮流的必要性。

target_sen.target_sen: Later on, when they tire of this and settle down with a board game, she's learning about the need to follow rules and take turns with a partner.
Later on, when they tire of this and settle down with a board game, she's learning about the need to follow rules and take turns with a partner.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“populations of ... have grown”意思是“...的人口增长了”。在原文中，"over half the people in the world now live in cities" 这句话表示“世界上超过一半的人现在住在城市里”，这意味着城市人口增长了。因此，答案是“cities”。

central_sentences.sentence: But we live in changing times, and Whitebread is mindful of a worldwide decline in play, pointing out that over half the people in the world now live in cities.
central_sentences.translation: 但我们生活在变化的时代，怀特布雷德意识到全球范围内游戏的减少，他指出现在世界上超过一半的人口居住在城市。

target_sen.target_sen: But we live in changing times, and Whitebread is mindful of a worldwide decline in play, pointing out that over half the people in the world now live in cities.
But we live in changing times, and Whitebread is mindful of a worldwide decline in play, pointing out that over half the people in the world now live in cities.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“fear of”对应原文中的“perceptions of risk to do with”，具体的恐惧对象是“traffic”和“crime”。因此，正确答案是“traffic/crime”。

central_sentences.sentence: Outdoor play is curtailed by perceptions of risk to do with traffic, as well as parents increased wish to protect their children from being the victims of crime, and by the emphasis on earlier is better which is leading to greater competition in academic learning and schools.
central_sentences.translation: 户外游戏受到了限制，这是由于人们对交通风险的感知，以及父母希望保护孩子免受犯罪侵害的愿望增强，还有人们认为"越早越好"的观念导致学术学习和学校之间的竞争加剧。

target_sen.target_sen: Outdoor play is curtailed by perceptions of risk to do with traffic, as well as parents increased wish to protect their children from being the victims of crime, and by the emphasis on earlier is better which is leading to greater competition in academic learning and schools.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "fear of" 对应原文中的 "perceptions of risk"，而具体的风险对象是 "traffic" 和 "crime"。因此，正确答案是 "crime/traffic"。这些词汇直接对应了父母对孩子户外活动的具体恐惧对象。

central_sentences.sentence: Outdoor play is curtailed by perceptions of risk to do with traffic, as well as parents increased wish to protect their children from being the victims of crime, and by the emphasis on earlier is better which is leading to greater competition in academic learning and schools.
central_sentences.translation: 户外游戏被限制是由于对交通的风险感知，以及父母希望保护孩子不成为犯罪受害者的愿望增强，还有"越早越好"的观念导致学术学习和学校中的竞争加剧。

target_sen.target_sen: Outdoor play is curtailed by perceptions of risk to do with traffic, as well as parents increased wish to protect their children from being the victims of crime, and by the emphasis on earlier is better which is leading to greater competition in academic learning and schools.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“increased”在原文中对应的是“greater”，“in schools”在原文中对应的是“in academic learning and schools”。通过这些同义替换词，我们可以定位到答案句。答案句中明确提到“greater competition in academic learning and schools”，这与题干中的“increased 【 】, in schools”完全对应，因此答案是“competition”。

central_sentences.sentence: Outdoor play is curtailed by perceptions of risk to do with traffic, as well as parents increased wish to protect their children from being the victims of crime, and by the emphasis on earlier is better which is leading to greater competition in academic learning and schools.
central_sentences.translation: 户外游戏因交通风险的担忧而受到限制，同时父母也更希望保护孩子免受犯罪侵害，再加上人们强调"越早越好"的观念，这导致学术学习和学校中的竞争越来越激烈。

target_sen.target_sen: Outdoor play is curtailed by perceptions of risk to do with traffic, as well as parents increased wish to protect their children from being the victims of crime, and by the emphasis on earlier is better which is leading to greater competition in academic learning and schools.

Outdoor play is curtailed by perceptions of risk to do with traffic, as well as parents increased wish to protect their children from being the victims of crime, and by the emphasis on earlier is better which is leading to greater competition in academic learning and schools.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“difficult to find”在原文中对应的是“lack”，而“support new policies”对应的是“base policies on”。通过这些对应关系，我们可以确定原文中的句子“But what they often lack is the evidence to base policies on.”是答案句。句中明确指出缺乏的是“evidence”，因此正确答案是“evidence”。

central_sentences.sentence: But what they often lack is the evidence to base policies on.
central_sentences.translation: 但他们通常缺乏的是用来制定政策的证据。

target_sen.target_sen: But what they often lack is the evidence to base policies on.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是 "life"。题干中的 "impact" 和 "child" 是关键线索词，在原文中直接对应 "impact" 和 "child"。原文中的句子 "there is very little data on the impact it has on the child's later life" 明确提到游戏对孩子后期生活的影响，因此答案是 "life"。

central_sentences.sentence: Dr Jenny Gibson agrees, pointing out that although some of the steps in the puzzle of how and why play is important have been looked at, there is very little data on the impact it has on the child's later life.
central_sentences.translation: Jenny Gibson博士同意这一观点，她指出虽然已经研究了游戏为何重要以及如何重要这一谜题的一些步骤，但关于游戏对儿童后期生活的影响几乎没有数据。

target_sen.target_sen: Dr Jenny Gibson agrees, pointing out that although some of the steps in the puzzle of how and why play is important have been looked at, there is very little data on the impact it has on the child's later life.

Dr Jenny Gibson agrees, pointing out that although some of the steps in the puzzle of how and why play is important have been looked at, there is very little data on the impact it has on the child's later life.

groups.desc:
Questions 9-13

Choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text,
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information,
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: Children with good self-control are known to be likely to do well at school later on.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“Children with good self-control are known to be likely to do well at school later on.”在文章中得到了明确的支持。文章中提到“the ability to self-regulate has been shown to be a key predictor of academic performance”，这与题干中的“good self-control”和“do well at school”是同义表达。因此，答案是 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: If playful experiences do facilitate this aspect of development, say the researchers, it could be extremely significant for educational practices, because the ability to self-regulate has been shown to be a key predictor of academic performance.
central_sentences.translation: 研究人员表示，如果游戏经历确实能促进这方面的发展，那么这对教育实践来说可能极为重要，因为自我调节能力已被证明是学业表现的一个关键预测因素。

target_sen.target_sen: If playful experiences do facilitate this aspect of development, say the researchers, it could be extremely significant for educational practices, because the ability to self-regulate has been shown to be a key predictor of academic performance.

questions.title: The way a child plays may provide information about possible medical problems.
questions.analysis: 答案是 TRUE，因为题干中的 "The way a child plays may provide information about possible medical problems" 与原文中的 "observing children at play can give us important clues about their well-being and can even be useful in the diagnosis of neurodevelopmental disorders like autism" 意思相符。原文明确指出，通过观察孩子们的游戏行为，可以获得关于他们健康的重要线索，甚至可以用于诊断神经发育障碍（如自闭症），这与题干中的信息一致。

central_sentences.sentence: In my previous research, I investigated how observing children at play can give us important clues about their well-being and can even be useful in the diagnosis of neurodevelopmental disorders like autism.
central_sentences.translation: 在我之前的研究中，我探讨了观察儿童玩耍如何能为我们提供关于他们健康状况的重要线索，甚至可以用于诊断自闭症等神经发育障碍。

target_sen.target_sen: In my previous research, I investigated how observing children at play can give us important clues about their well-being and can even be useful in the diagnosis of neurodevelopmental disorders like autism.'

In my previous research, I investigated how observing children at play can give us important clues about their well-being and can even be useful in the diagnosis of neurodevelopmental disorders like autism.'

questions.title: Playing with dolls was found to benefit girls' writing more than boys' writing.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词“Playing with dolls”和“benefit”在原文中找到了对应的同义替换词“played with dolls”和“far more effective”。然而，题干中的“girls' writing more than boys' writing”在原文中没有找到对应的信息。原文只提到“children”，并没有区分性别。因此，题干中的信息在文章中没有被提及，正确答案是“NOT GIVEN”。

central_sentences.sentence: Children wrote longer and better-structured stories when they first played with dolls representing characters in the story.
central_sentences.translation: 当孩子们先用玩偶扮演故事中的角色时，他们写出了更长且结构更好的故事。

syn_ary.synonym: played with dolls representing characters in the story

target_sen.target_sen: Children wrote longer and better-structured stories when they first played with dolls representing characters in the story.
Children wrote longer and better-structured stories when they first played with dolls representing characters in the story.
Whitebread's recent research has involved developing a play-based approach to supporting children's writing.
'Many primary school children find writing difficult, but we showed in a previous study that a playful stimulus was far more effective than an instructional one.'

questions.title: Children had problems thinking up ideas when they first created the story with Lego.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“孩子们在用乐高创建故事时遇到了问题”，而原文明确指出“在用乐高搭建的过程中，整个项目的一年中没有一个孩子说过不知道写什么”。这与题干中的信息相反，因此正确答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: With the Lego building, however, not a single child said this through the whole year of the project.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，在使用乐高积木创作的过程中，整个项目年度内没有一个孩子说过这样的话。

syn_ary.synonym: didn't know what to write about

target_sen.target_sen: 'Many teachers commented that they had always previously had children saying they didn't know what to write about.
With the Lego building, however, not a single child said this through the whole year of the project.'

questions.title: People nowadays regard children's play as less significant than they did in the past.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“nowadays”（现在）、“less significant”（不那么重要）和“in the past”（过去）在原文中分别对应“recent decades”（最近几十年）、“importance of play has been lost”（游戏的重要性已经丧失）以及“trivial”和“negative”（微不足道和负面）。这些表达直接对应了题干所述内容，说明现今的人们确实认为儿童游戏不如过去重要。因此，答案为TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Somehow the importance of play has been lost in recent decades. It's regarded as something trivial, or even as something negative that contrasts with "work".
central_sentences.translation: 不知何故，在最近几十年里，游戏的重要性已经丧失了。它被认为是微不足道的，甚至是与"工作"相对立的负面事物。

target_sen.target_sen: 'Somehow the importance of play has been lost in recent decades.
It's regarded as something trivial, or even as something negative that contrasts with "work".

content.title: The growth of bike-sharing schemes around the world

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: The original idea for an urban bike-sharing scheme dates back to a summer's day in Amsterdam in 1965. Provo, the organisation that came up with the idea, was a group of Dutch activists who wanted to change society. They believed the scheme, which was known as the Witte Fietsenplan, was an answer to the perceived threats of air pollution and consumerism. In the centre of Amsterdam, they painted a small number of used bikes white. They also distributed leaflets describing the dangers of cars and inviting people to use the white bikes. The bikes were then left unlocked at various locations around the city, to be used by anyone in need of transport.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Luud Schimmelpennink, a Dutch industrial engineer who still lives and cycles in Amsterdam, was heavily involved in the original scheme. He recalls how the scheme succeeded in attracting a great deal of attention—particularly when it came to publicising Provo's aims—but struggled to get off the ground. The police were opposed to Provo's initiatives and almost as soon as the white bikes were distributed around the city, they removed them. However, for Schimmelpennink and for bike-sharing schemes in general, this was just the beginning. 'The first Witte Fietsenplan was just a symbolic thing,' he says. 'We painted a few bikes white, that was all. Things got more serious when I became a member of the Amsterdam city council two years later.'

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Schimmelpennink seized this opportunity to present a more elaborate Witte Fietsenplan to the city council. 'My idea was that the municipality of Amsterdam would distribute 10,000 white bikes over the city, for everyone to use,' he explains. 'I made serious calculations. It turned out that a white bicycle—per person, per kilometer—would cost the municipality only 10% of what it contributed to public transport per person per kilometre.' Nevertheless, the council unanimously rejected the plan. 'They said that the bicycle belongs to the past. They saw a glorious future for the car,' says Schimmelpennink. But he was not in the least discouraged.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Schimmelpennink never stopped believing in bike-sharing, and in the mid-90s, two Danes asked for his help to set up a system in Copenhagen. The result was the world's first large-scale bike-share programme. It worked on a deposit: 'You dropped a coin in the bike and when you returned it, you got your money back.' After setting up the Danish system, Schimmelpennink decided to try his luck again in the Netherlands—and this time he succeeded in arousing the interest of the Dutch Ministry of Transport. 'Times had changed,' he recalls. 'People had become more environmentally conscious, and the Danish experiment had proved that bike-sharing was a real possibility.' A new Witte Fietsenplan was launched in 1999 in Amsterdam. However, riding a white bike was no longer free; it cost one guilder per trip and payment was made with a chip card developed by the Dutch bank Postbank. Schimmelpennink designed conspicuous, sturdy white bikes locked in special racks which could be opened with the chip card—the plan started with 250 bikes, distributed over five stations.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Theo Molenaar, who was a system designer for the project, worked alongside Schimmelpennink. 'I remember when we were testing the bike racks, he announced that he had already designed better ones. But of course, we had to go through with the ones we had.' The system, however, was prone to vandalism and theft. 'After every weekend there would always be a couple of bikes missing,' Molenaar says. 'I really have no idea what people did with them, because they could instantly be recognised as white bikes.' But the biggest blow came when Postbank decided to abolish the chip card, because it wasn't profitable. 'That chip card was pivotal to the system,' Molenaar says. 'To continue the project we would have needed to set up another system, but the business partner had lost interest.'

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Schimmelpennink was disappointed, but—characteristically—not for long. In 2002 he got a call from the French advertising corporation JCDecaux, who wanted to set up his bike-sharing scheme in Vienna. 'That went really well. After Vienna, they set up a system in Lyon. Then in 2007, Paris followed. That was a decisive moment in the history of bike-sharing.' The huge and unexpected success of the Parisian bike-sharing programme, which now boasts more than 20,000 bicycles, inspired cities all over the world to set up their own schemes, all modelled on Schimmelpennink's. 'It's wonderful that this happened,' he says. 'But financially I didn't really benefit from it, because I never filed for a patent.'

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: In Amsterdam today, 38% of all trips are made by bike and, along with Copenhagen, it is regarded as one of the two most cycle-friendly capitals in the world—but the city never got another Witte Fietsenplan. Molenaar believes this may be because everybody in Amsterdam already has a bike. Schimmelpennink, however, cannot see that this changes Amsterdam's need for a bike-sharing scheme. 'People who travel on the underground don't carry their bikes around. But often they need additional transport to reach their final destination.' Although he thinks it is strange that a city like Amsterdam does not have a successful bike-sharing scheme, he is optimistic about the future. 'In the '60s we didn't stand a chance because people were prepared to give their lives to keep cars in the city. But that mentality has totally changed. Today everybody longs for cities that are not dominated by cars.'

groups.desc:
Questions 14-18

Reading Passage 2 has seven paragraphs, A-G.
Which paragraph contains the following information?
Choose the correct answer.
NB You may use any letter more than once.

questions.title: a description of how people misused a bike-sharing scheme
questions.analysis: 段落E最直接地描述了自行车共享计划被滥用的情况，包括破坏和盗窃行为。题目中的“misused”在原文中对应的同义词是“vandalism”和“theft”，以及“missing”。这些词汇明确描述了自行车共享计划被滥用的具体情况，因此段落E是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: The system, however, was prone to vandalism and theft.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，这个系统容易受到故意破坏和盗窃。

central_sentences.sentence: After every weekend there would always be a couple of bikes missing,
central_sentences.translation: 每个周末过后，总会有几辆自行车丢失。

target_sen.target_sen: The system, however, was prone to vandalism and theft.
The system, however, was prone to vandalism and theft.

Theo Molenaar, who was a system designer for the project, worked alongside Schimmelpennink.

'That chip card was pivotal to the system,' Molenaar says.

'To continue the project we would have needed to set up another system, but the business partner had lost interest.'

questions.title: an explanation of why a proposed bike-sharing scheme was turned down
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是段落C，因为段落C明确解释了为什么提议的共享单车计划被拒绝。市议会认为自行车属于过去，他们看到了汽车的光明未来。这与题干中的信息完全对应。

central_sentences.sentence: Nevertheless, the council unanimously rejected the plan. 'They said that the bicycle belongs to the past. They saw a glorious future for the car,' says Schimmelpennink.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，市议会一致否决了这个计划。Schimmelpennink说："他们说自行车属于过去。他们看到了汽车的光明未来。"

target_sen.target_sen: Nevertheless, the council unanimously rejected the plan.
'They said that the bicycle belongs to the past.

questions.title: a reference to a person being unable to profit from their work
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是段落 F，因为题干中的“a person being unable to profit from their work”在段落 F 中有直接对应的表达：“But financially I didn’t really benefit from it, because I never filed for a patent.” 这句话明确说明了 Schimmelpennink 没有从他的工作中获得经济利益，这与题干信息完全一致。

central_sentences.sentence: But financially I didn't really benefit from it, because I never filed for a patent.
central_sentences.translation: 但是从财务上来说，我并没有真正从中受益，因为我从未申请专利。

target_sen.target_sen: 'But financially I didn't really benefit from it, because I never filed for a patent.'

The huge and unexpected success of the Parisian bike-sharing programme, which now boasts more than 20,000 bicycles, inspired cities all over the world to set up their own schemes, all modelled on Schimmelpennink's.
'But financially I didn't really benefit from it, because I never filed for a patent.'

questions.title: an explanation of the potential savings a bike- sharing scheme would bring
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是段落C，因为段落C中明确提到了共享单车计划可能带来的节省。Schimmelpennink提到他做了详细的计算，结果显示每人每公里使用白色自行车的成本仅为公共交通成本的10%。这正是对共享单车计划可能带来的节省的解释。

central_sentences.sentence: It turned out that a white bicycle - per person, per kilometer - would cost the municipality only 10% of what it contributed to public transport per person per kilometre.
central_sentences.translation: 结果表明，每人每公里使用白色自行车的成本只有公共交通每人每公里成本的10%。

target_sen.target_sen: It turned out that a white bicycle - per person, per kilometer - would cost the municipality only 10% of what it contributed to public transport per person per kilometre.'
It turned out that a white bicycle - per person, per kilometer - would cost the municipality only 10% of what it contributed to public transport per person per kilometre.'

questions.title: a reference to the problems a bike-sharing scheme was intended to solve
questions.analysis: 这道题目要求找到自行车共享计划旨在解决的问题。段落A明确提到“他们认为这个计划是对空气污染和消费主义威胁的回应”，这与题目中的“problems a bike-sharing scheme was intended to solve”直接对应。因此，段落A是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: They believed the scheme, which was known as the Witte Fietsenplan, was an answer to the perceived threats of air pollution and consumerism.
central_sentences.translation: 他们认为这个被称为"白色自行车计划"的方案是解决空气污染和消费主义这些被认为的威胁的答案。

target_sen.target_sen: They believed the scheme, which was known as the Witte Fietsenplan, was an answer to the perceived threats of air pollution and consumerism.
They believed the scheme, which was known as the Witte Fietsenplan, was an answer to the perceived threats of air pollution and consumerism.

groups.title: Which TWO of the following statements are made in the text about the Amsterdambike-sharing scheme of 1999?
groups.desc:
Questions 19 and 20

Choose TWO letters, A-E.

collect_option.title: Which TWO of the following statements are made in the text about the Amsterdambike-sharing scheme of 1999?

list.text: It was initially opposed by a government department.
It failed when a partner in the scheme withdrew support.
It aimed to be more successful than the Copenhagen scheme.
It was made possible by a change in people's attitudes.
It attracted interest from a range of bike designers.

questions.analysis: 根据题目中的时间点“1999”定位到原文D段和E段。D段提到“Times had changed... People had become more environmentally conscious”，指出当时人们的环保意识已经增强，这种大众态度的转变（change in people’s attitudes）使得1999年的计划得以启动，对应选项D。随后在E段中，文章描述了该计划失败的原因：“But the biggest blow came when Postbank decided to abolish the chip card … the business partner had lost interest”，说明由于合作伙伴Postbank决定废除芯片卡系统并失去兴趣，导致项目因为失去伙伴支持而难以为继，对应选项B。避坑：本题容易误选A或E。选项A称政府部门最初反对，但原文D段明确提到1999年的计划成功引起了荷兰交通部的兴趣，早期的反对意见主要出现在1965年的首次尝试中，属于张冠李戴。选项E提到吸引了众多自行车设计师，而原文仅提到Theo Molenaar与Schimmelpennink两人的合作，并未提及其他设计师的参与。考生在做题时应严格区分不同历史阶段的细节，避免混淆初次尝试与1999年方案的背景信息。

central_sentences.sentence: But the biggest blow came when Postbank decided to abolish the chip card, because it wasn't profitable. 'That chip card was pivotal to the system,' Molenaar says. 'To continue the project we would have needed to set up another system, but the business partner had lost interest.'
central_sentences.translation: 但最大的打击是当邮政银行决定取消芯片卡，因为它不再有利可图。莫勒纳尔说："那张芯片卡对系统至关重要。要继续这个项目，我们需要建立另一个系统，但商业伙伴已经失去了兴趣。"

central_sentences.sentence: Times had changed,' he recalls. 'People had become more environmentally conscious, and the Danish experiment had proved that bike-sharing was a real possibility' A
central_sentences.translation: 他回忆说："时代变了。人们变得更有环保意识，而丹麦的实验证明了自行车共享是一个真实的可能性。"

target_sen.target_sen: Theo Molenaar, who was a system designer for the project, worked alongside Schimmelpennink.
'To continue the project we would have needed to set up another system, but the business partner had lost interest.'
The system, however, was prone to vandalism and theft.

syn_ary.synonym: The system, however, was prone to vandalism and theft

target_sen.target_sen: The system, however, was prone to vandalism and theft.

syn_ary.synonym: But the biggest blow came when Postbank decided to abolish the chip card, because it wasn't profitable

target_sen.target_sen: But the biggest blow came when Postbank decided to abolish the chip card, because it wasn't profitable.

syn_ary.synonym: That chip card was pivotal to the system

target_sen.target_sen: 'That chip card was pivotal to the system,' Molenaar says.

syn_ary.synonym: To continue the project we would have needed to set up another system, but the business partner had lost interest

target_sen.target_sen: 'To continue the project we would have needed to set up another system, but the business partner had lost interest.'

groups.title: Which TWO of the following statements are made in the text about Amsterdam today?
groups.desc:
Questions 21 and 22

Choose TWO letters, A-E.

collect_option.title: Which TWO of the following statements are made in the text about Amsterdam today?

list.text: The majority of residents would like to prevent all cars from entering the city.
There is little likelihood of the city having another bike-sharing scheme.
More trips in the city are made by bike than by any other form of transport.
A bike-sharing scheme would benefit residents who use public transport.
The city has a reputation as a place that welcomes cyclists.

questions.analysis:
根据题目中的关键词「Amsterdam today」,结合原文段落中的相关信息,可以得出以下分析:

D选项:原文中提到'People who travel on the underground don't carry their bikes around. But often they need additional transport to reach their final destination.'说明bike-sharing scheme可以帮助使用公共交通工具的人解决'最后一公里'的问题,属于正确选项。

E选项:原文中描述Amsterdam是'one of the two most cycle-friendly capitals in the world',说明这座城市对骑自行车的人是友好的,也是正确选项。

其他选项并未得到原文的明确支持,因此不是正确选项。

central_sentences.sentence: People who travel on the underground don't carry their bikes around. But often they need additional transport to reach their final destination.
central_sentences.translation: 乘坐地铁的人不会随身携带自行车。但他们通常需要额外的交通工具来到达最终目的地。

central_sentences.sentence: In Amsterdam today, 38% of all trips are made by bike and, along with Copenhagen, it is regarded as one of the two most cycle-friendly capitals in the world
central_sentences.translation: 如今在阿姆斯特丹，38%的出行是通过自行车完成的，与哥本哈根一起，它被认为是世界上最适合骑行的两个首都之一。

target_sen.target_sen: In Amsterdam today, 38% of all trips are made by bike and, along with Copenhagen, it is regarded as one of the two most cycle-friendly capitals in the world - but the city never got another Witte Fietsenplan.

In Amsterdam today, 38% of all trips are made by bike and, along with Copenhagen, it is regarded as one of the two most cycle-friendly capitals in the world - but the city never got another Witte Fietsenplan.

questions.analysis:

groups.title: The first urban bike-sharing scheme
groups.desc:
Questions 23-26

Complete the summary below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
groups.collect:
The first bike-sharing scheme was the idea of the Dutch group Provo. The people who belonged to this group were $657$. They were concerned about damage to the environment and about$658$ , and believed that the bike-sharing scheme would draw attention to these issues. As well as painting some bikes white, they handed out $659$that condemned the use of cars.

However, the scheme was not a great success: almost as quickly as Provo left the bikes around the city, the $660$took them away. According to Schimmelpennink, the scheme was intended to be symbolic. The idea was to get people thinking about the issues.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "The people who belonged to this group" 对应文章中的 "a group of Dutch activists"。这里的 "activists" 直接回答了题干中的空格。题干中的 "group" 和 "belonged to" 在文章中分别对应 "group" 和 "was"，通过这些线索词可以定位到答案句。答案 "activists" 是对这群人的具体描述，符合题意。

central_sentences.sentence: Provo, the organisation that came up with the idea, was a group of Dutch activists who wanted to change society.
central_sentences.translation: Provo，提出这个想法的组织，是一群想要改变社会的荷兰积极分子。

target_sen.target_sen: Provo, the organisation that came up with the idea, was a group of Dutch activists who wanted to change society.
Provo, the organisation that came up with the idea, was a group of Dutch activists who wanted to change society.
They believed the scheme, which was known as the Witte Fietsenplan, was an answer to the perceived threats of air pollution and consumerism.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“damage to the environment”对应原文中的“air pollution”，而“about”对应原文中的“consumerism”。因此，题干中空格处应填入“consumerism”，因为它与题干中的“about”直接对应，并且与“damage to the environment”并列出现，符合题干要求。

central_sentences.sentence: They believed the scheme, which was known as the Witte Fietsenplan, was an answer to the perceived threats of air pollution and consumerism.
central_sentences.translation: 他们认为这个被称为白色自行车计划（Witte Fietsenplan）的方案是应对空气污染和消费主义这两个被认为的威胁的答案。

target_sen.target_sen: They believed the scheme, which was known as the Witte Fietsenplan, was an answer to the perceived threats of air pollution and consumerism.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "handed out" 对应原文中的 "distributed"，"condemned the use of cars" 对应原文中的 "describing the dangers of cars"。通过这些对应关系，我们可以确定答案所在的句子是 "They also distributed leaflets describing the dangers of cars and inviting people to use the white bikes."。因此，答案是 "leaflets"，因为它是被分发的物品，符合题干的要求。

central_sentences.sentence: They also distributed leaflets describing the dangers of cars and inviting people to use the white bikes.
central_sentences.translation: 他们还分发了描述汽车危害并邀请人们使用白色自行车的传单。

syn_ary.synonym: painted a small number of used bikes white

target_sen.target_sen: In the centre of Amsterdam, they painted a small number of used bikes white.
They also distributed leaflets describing the dangers of cars and inviting people to use the white bikes.
They also distributed leaflets describing the dangers of cars and inviting people to use the white bikes.

questions.analysis: 答案是“police”，因为题干中的“left the bikes around the city”对应文章中的“distributed around the city”，“took them away”对应文章中的“removed them”。通过这些对应关系，我们可以确定文章中提到的移走自行车的主体是“the police”。

central_sentences.sentence: The police were opposed to Provo's initiatives and almost as soon as the white bikes were distributed around the city, they removed them.
central_sentences.translation: 警察反对Provo的倡议，几乎在白色自行车分布在城市各处的同时，他们就把这些自行车移走了。

target_sen.target_sen: The police were opposed to Provo's initiatives and almost as soon as the white bikes were distributed around the city, they removed them.

locating_words.locating_word: left the bikes around the city

target_sen.target_sen: The police were opposed to Provo's initiatives and almost as soon as the white bikes were distributed around the city, they removed them.

content.title: Motivational factors and the hospitality industry

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: A critical ingredient in the success of hotels is developing and maintaining superior performance from their employees. How is that accomplished? What Human Resource Management (HRM) practices should organizations invest in to acquire and retain great employees?

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Some hotels aim to provide superior working conditions for their employees. The idea originated from workplaces usually in the non-service sector—that emphasized fun and enjoyment as part of work-life balance. By contrast, the service sector, and more specifically hotels, has traditionally not extended these practices to address basic employee needs, such as good working conditions.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Pfeffer (1994) emphasizes that in order to succeed in a global business environment, organizations must make investment in Human Resource Management (HRM) to allow them to acquire employees who possess better skills and capabilities than their competitors. This investment will be to their competitive advantage. Despite this recognition of the importance of employee development, the hospitality industry has historically been dominated by underdeveloped HR practices (Lucas, 2002).

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Lucas also points out that 'the substance of HRM practices does not appear to be designed to foster constructive relations with employees or to represent a managerial approach that enables developing and drawing out the full potential of people, even though employees may be broadly satisfied with many aspects of their work' (Lucas, 2002). In addition, or maybe as a result, high employee turnover has been a recurring problem throughout the hospitality industry. Among the many cited reasons are low compensation, inadequate benefits, poor working conditions and compromised employee morale and attitudes (Maroudas et al., 2008).

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Ng and Sorensen (2008) demonstrated that when managers provide recognition to employees, motivate employees to work together, and remove obstacles preventing effective performance, employees feel more obligated to stay with the company. This was succinctly summarized by Michel et al. (2013): '[P]roviding support to employees gives them the confidence to perform their jobs better and the motivation to stay with the organization.' Hospitality organizations can therefore enhance employee motivation and retention through the development and improvement of their working conditions. These conditions are inherently linked to the working environment.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: While it seems likely that employees' reactions to their job characteristics could be affected by a predisposition to view their work environment negatively, no evidence exists to support this hypothesis (Spector et al., 2000). However, given the opportunity, many people will find something to complain about in relation to their workplace (Poulston, 2009). There is a strong link between the perceptions of employees and particular factors of their work environment that are separate from the work itself, including company policies, salary and vacations.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Such conditions are particularly troubling for the luxury hotel market, where high-quality service, requiring a sophisticated approach to HRM, is recognized as a critical source of competitive advantage (Maroudas et al., 2008). In a real sense, the services of hotel employees represent their industry (Schneider and Bowen, 1993). This representation has commonly been limited to guest experiences. This suggests that there has been a dichotomy between the guest environment provided in luxury hotels and the working conditions of their employees.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: It is therefore essential for hotel management to develop HRM practices that enable them to inspire and retain competent employees. This requires an understanding of what motivates employees at different levels of management and different stages of their careers (Enz and Siguaw, 2000). This implies that it is beneficial for hotel managers to understand what practices are most favorable to increase employee satisfaction and retention.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Herzberg (1966) proposes that people have two major types of needs, the first being extrinsic motivation factors relating to the context in which work is performed, rather than the work itself. These include working conditions and job security. When these factors are unfavorable, job dissatisfaction may result. Significantly, though, just fulfilling these needs does not result in satisfaction, but only in the reduction of dissatisfaction (Maroudas et al., 2008).

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: Employees also have intrinsic motivation needs or motivators, which include such factors as achievement and recognition. Unlike extrinsic factors, motivator factors may ideally result in job satisfaction (Maroudas et al., 2008). Herzberg's (1966) theory discusses the need for a 'balance' of these two types of needs.

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: The impact of fun as a motivating factor at work has also been explored. For example, Tews, Michel and Stafford (2013) conducted a study focusing on staff from a chain of themed restaurants in the United States. It was found that fun activities had a favorable impact on performance and manager support for fun had a favorable impact in reducing turnover. Their findings support the view that fun may indeed have a beneficial effect, but the framing of that fun must be carefully aligned with both organizational goals and employee characteristics. 'Managers must learn how to achieve the delicate balance of allowing employees the freedom to enjoy themselves at work while simultaneously maintaining high levels of performance' (Tews et al., 2013).

paragraphs.label: L
paragraphs.text: Deery (2008) has recommended several actions that can be adopted at the organizational level to retain good staff as well as assist in balancing work and family life. Those particularly appropriate to the hospitality industry include allowing adequate breaks during the working day, staff functions that involve families, and providing health and well-being opportunities.

groups.title: List of Researchers
groups.desc:
Questions 27-31
Look at the following statements and the list of researchers below.
Match each statement with the correct researcher, A-F.

Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.
NB You may use any letter more than once.

questions.title: Hotel managers need to know what would encourage good staff to remain.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Hotel managers need to know what would encourage good staff to remain" 与原文中的 "hotel management" 和 "requires an understanding of what motivates employees at different levels of management and different stages of their careers" 对应。Enz 和 Siguaw 的研究强调了酒店管理层需要了解什么能激励员工并留住他们，因此正确答案是 E. Enz and Siguaw。

central_sentences.sentence: This requires an understanding of what motivates employees at different levels of management and different stages of their careers (Enz and Siguaw, 2000).
central_sentences.translation: 这需要了解在不同管理层级和职业阶段的员工的激励因素（Enz和Siguaw，2000）。

target_sen.target_sen: It is therefore essential for hotel management to develop HRM practices that enable them to inspire and retain competent employees.
It is therefore essential for hotel management to develop HRM practices that enable them to inspire and retain competent employees.

questions.title: The actions of managers may make staff feel they shouldn't move to a different employer.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 D: Ng and Sorensen。题干中的“actions of managers”对应原文中的“when managers provide recognition to employees, motivate employees to work together, and remove obstacles preventing effective performance”，而“staff feel they shouldn't move to a different employer”对应原文中的“employees feel more obligated to stay with the company”。因此，Ng 和 Sorensen 的研究表明，管理者的行为可以让员工感到不应该换工作。

central_sentences.sentence: Ng and Sorensen (2008) demonstrated that when managers provide recognition to employees, motivate employees to work together, and remove obstacles preventing effective performance, employees feel more obligated to stay with the company.
central_sentences.translation: Ng和Sorensen（2008）证明，当管理者对员工给予认可、激励员工团队合作，并消除阻碍有效表现的障碍时，员工会感到更有义务留在公司。

syn_ary.synonym: managers provide recognition to employees, motivate employees to work together, and remove obstacles preventing effective performance

target_sen.target_sen: Ng and Sorensen (2008) demonstrated that when managers provide recognition to employees, motivate employees to work together, and remove obstacles preventing effective performance, employees feel more obligated to stay with the company.

questions.title: Little is done in the hospitality industry to help workers improve their skills.
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是B. Lucas。题干中的“Little is done in the hospitality industry to help workers improve their skills”意思是酒店行业在帮助员工提升技能方面做得很少。文章中Lucas的观点明确指出，人力资源管理实践并没有设计成促进员工关系或开发员工潜力的方式，这与题干的意思相符。

central_sentences.sentence: Lucas also points out that 'the substance of HRM practices does not appear to be designed to foster constructive relations with employees or to represent a managerial approach that enables developing and drawing out the full potential of people, even though employees may be broadly satisfied with many aspects of their work' (Lucas, 2002).
central_sentences.translation: Lucas还指出，"人力资源管理实践的实质似乎并不是为了促进与员工的建设性关系，也不是为了代表一种能够开发和发挥员工全部潜力的管理方法，尽管员工可能对工作的许多方面大体满意"（Lucas，2002年）。

syn_ary.synonym: does not appear to be designed

target_sen.target_sen: Lucas also points out that 'the substance of HRM practices does not appear to be designed to foster constructive relations with employees or to represent a managerial approach that enables developing and drawing out the full potential of people, even though employees may be broadly satisfied with many aspects of their work' (Lucas, 2002).
In addition, or may be as a result, high employee turnover has been a recurring problem throughout the hospitality industry.

syn_ary.synonym: developing and drawing out the full potential of people

target_sen.target_sen: Lucas also points out that 'the substance of HRM practices does not appear to be designed to foster constructive relations with employees or to represent a managerial approach that enables developing and drawing out the full potential of people, even though employees may be broadly satisfied with many aspects of their work' (Lucas, 2002).

questions.title: Staff are less likely to change jobs if cooperation is encouraged.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 D. Ng and Sorensen。题干中的 "less likely to change jobs" 对应原文中的 "feel more obligated to stay with the company"，而 "cooperation is encouraged" 对应 "motivate employees to work together"。因此，最直接对应的答案是 D. Ng and Sorensen。

central_sentences.sentence: Ng and Sorensen (2008) demonstrated that when managers provide recognition to employees, motivate employees to work together, and remove obstacles preventing effective performance, employees feel more obligated to stay with the company.
central_sentences.translation: Ng和Sorensen（2008）证明，当管理者对员工给予认可、激励员工合作并消除阻碍有效表现的障碍时，员工会感到更有义务留在公司。

target_sen.target_sen: Ng and Sorensen (2008) demonstrated that when managers provide recognition to employees, motivate employees to work together, and remove obstacles preventing effective performance, employees feel more obligated to stay with the company.

questions.title: Dissatisfaction with pay is not the only reason why hospitality workers change jobs.
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是C. Maroudas et al.。题干提到“对薪酬不满不是酒店业员工换工作的唯一原因”，而在原文中，Maroudas等人明确列举了多个导致酒店业员工高流动率的原因，包括低薪酬、福利不足、工作条件差和员工士气和态度受损。这与题干的意思完全吻合。

central_sentences.sentence: Among the many cited reasons are low compensation, inadequate benefits, poor working conditions and compromised employee morale and attitudes (Maroudas et al., 2008).
central_sentences.translation: 据引述，造成这种情况的原因有很多，包括低薪酬、福利不足、工作条件差以及员工士气和态度受损（Maroudas等人，2008年）。

target_sen.target_sen: Among the many cited reasons are low compensation, inadequate benefits, poor working conditions and compromised employee morale and attitudes (Maroudas et al., 2008).
Among the many cited reasons are low compensation, inadequate benefits, poor working conditions and compromised employee morale and attitudes (Maroudas et al., 2008).
In addition, or may be as a result, high employee turnover has been a recurring problem throughout the hospitality industry.

groups.desc:
Questions 32-35

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
Write
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

questions.title: One reason for high staff turnover in the hospitality industry is poor morale.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "high staff turnover" 和 "poor morale" 在原文中分别对应 "high employee turnover" 和 "compromised employee morale"。原文明确指出，员工高流动率的原因之一是受损的员工士气。因此，题干中的陈述是正确的，答案为 YES。

central_sentences.sentence: In addition, or may be as a result, high employee turnover has been a recurring problem throughout the hospitality industry. Among the many cited reasons are low compensation, inadequate benefits, poor working conditions and compromised employee morale and attitudes (Maroudas et al., 2008).
central_sentences.translation: 此外，或许正是由于这个原因，高员工流动率一直是酒店业反复出现的问题。在众多被提及的原因中，包括低薪酬、福利不足、工作条件差以及员工士气和态度受损（Maroudas等人，2008）。

target_sen.target_sen: In addition, or may be as a result, high employee turnover has been a recurring problem throughout the hospitality industry.
Among the many cited reasons are low compensation, inadequate benefits, poor working conditions and compromised employee morale and attitudes (Maroudas et al., 2008).

questions.title: Research has shown that staff have a tendency to dislike their workplace.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Research has shown that staff have a tendency to dislike their workplace”意思是“研究表明员工倾向于不喜欢他们的工作场所”。而原文中明确指出“没有证据支持员工倾向于负面看待他们的工作环境”，这与题干的陈述相反。因此，正确答案是“NO / FALSE”。

central_sentences.sentence: While it seems likely that employees' reactions to their job characteristics could be affected by a predisposition to view their work environment negatively, no evidence exists to support this hypothesis (Spector et al., 2000).
central_sentences.translation: 虽然员工对工作特征的反应可能会受到倾向于负面看待工作环境的影响，但没有证据支持这一假设（Spector等人，2000年）。

target_sen.target_sen: While it seems likely that employees' reactions to their job characteristics could be affected by a predisposition to view their work environment negatively, no evidence exists to support this hypothesis (Spector et al., 2000).

While it seems likely that employees' reactions to their job characteristics could be affected by a predisposition to view their work environment negatively, no evidence exists to support this hypothesis (Spector et al., 2000).

While it seems likely that employees' reactions to their job characteristics could be affected by a predisposition to view their work environment negatively, no evidence exists to support this hypothesis (Spector et al., 2000).
There is a strong link between the perceptions of employees and particular factors of their work environment that are separate from the work itself, including company policies, salary and vacations.

questions.title: An improvement in working conditions and job security makes staff satisfied with their jobs.
questions.analysis: 题干中的陈述是“改善工作条件和工作安全使员工对工作满意”，而原文中明确指出“仅仅满足这些需求并不会导致满意，而只是减少不满意”。这说明题干的陈述是错误的，因此正确答案是NO。

central_sentences.sentence: Significantly, though, just fulfilling these needs does not result in satisfaction, but only in the reduction of is satisfaction (Maroudas et al., 2008
central_sentences.translation: 然而，值得注意的是，仅仅满足这些需求并不会导致满意，而只是减少不满意（Maroudas等人，2008年）。

target_sen.target_sen: These include working conditions and job security.
These include working conditions and job security.
When these factors are unfavorable, job dissatisfaction may result.
Significantly, though, just fulfilling these needs does not result in satisfaction, but only in the reduction of is satisfaction (Maroudas et al., 2008).

questions.title: Staff should be allowed to choose when they take breaks during the working day.
questions.analysis: 题干问是否员工可以选择在工作日何时休息，而文章中只提到允许员工在工作日休息，但没有提到是否可以自己选择休息时间。因此，正确答案是“NOT GIVEN”，因为文章没有提供足够的信息来确认或否定这一点。

central_sentences.sentence: Those particularly appropriate to the hospitality industry include allowing adequate breaks during the working day, staff functions that involve families, and providing health and well-being opportunities.
central_sentences.translation: 那些特别适合酒店业的做法包括在工作日允许适当的休息时间，组织包括家庭成员在内的员工活动，以及提供健康和福利机会。

target_sen.target_sen: Those particularly appropriate to the hospitality industry include allowing adequate breaks during the working day, staff functions that involve families, and providing health and well-being opportunities.

Those particularly appropriate to the hospitality industry include allowing adequate breaks during the working day, staff functions that involve families, and providing health and well-being opportunities.

groups.title: Fun at work
groups.desc:
Questions 36-40

Complete the summary below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
groups.collect: Tews, Michel and Stafford carried out research on staff in an American chain of $670$. They discovered that activities designed for staff to have fun improved their $671$, and that management involvement led to lower staff $672$ . They also found that the activities needed to fit with both the company's $673$ and the $674$of the staff. A balance was required between a degree of freedom and maintaining work standards.

questions.analysis: 题干中的关键词“Tews, Michel and Stafford”、“research”、“staff”和“American chain of”在原文中分别对应“conducted a study”、“staff”和“chain of ... in the United States”。通过这些关键词的对应关系，我们可以确定答案句为“Tews, Michel and Stafford (2013) conducted a study focusing on staff from a chain of themed restaurants in the United States.”。从这句话中可以看出，研究对象是“a chain of themed restaurants”，因此答案是“restaurants”。

central_sentences.sentence: Tews, Michel and Stafford (2013) conducted a study focusing on staff from a chain of themed restaurants in the United States.
central_sentences.translation: Tews、Michel和Stafford（2013年）对美国一家连锁主题餐厅的员工进行了研究。

target_sen.target_sen: For example, Tews, Michel and Stafford (2013) conducted a study focusing on staff from a chain of themed restaurants in the United States.
For example, Tews, Michel and Stafford (2013) conducted a study focusing on staff from a chain of themed restaurants in the United States.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "activities designed for staff to have fun" 对应原文中的 "fun activities"，"improved their" 对应 "had a favorable impact on"，"management involvement" 对应 "manager support for fun"，"led to lower staff" 对应 "had a favorable impact in reducing"。通过这些同义替换词，我们可以确定答案句中的 "performance" 是正确答案，因为它直接对应了题干中的 "improved their"。

central_sentences.sentence: It was found that fun activities had a favorable impact on performance and manager support for fun had a favorable impact in reducing turnover.
central_sentences.translation: 研究发现，有趣的活动对员工的表现产生了积极影响，而管理层对这些有趣活动的支持则有助于降低员工流失率。

target_sen.target_sen: It was found that fun activities had a favorable impact on performance and manager support for fun had a favorable impact in reducing turnover.

locating_words.locating_word: activities designed for staff to have fun

target_sen.target_sen: It was found that fun activities had a favorable impact on performance and manager support for fun had a favorable impact in reducing turnover.
It was found that fun activities had a favorable impact on performance and manager support for fun had a favorable impact in reducing turnover.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "turnover"。题干中的 "activities designed for staff to have fun" 对应原文中的 "fun activities"，"management involvement" 对应 "manager support for fun"，"lower staff" 对应 "reducing turnover"。通过这些对应关系，可以确定答案句中的 "reducing turnover" 是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: It was found that fun activities had a favorable impact on performance and manager support for fun had a favorable impact in reducing turnover.
central_sentences.translation: 研究发现，有趣的活动对员工表现有积极影响，而管理者对有趣活动的支持则有利于减少员工流失。

target_sen.target_sen: It was found that fun activities had a favorable impact on performance and manager support for fun had a favorable impact in reducing turnover.

target_sen.target_sen: It was found that fun activities had a favorable impact on performance and manager support for fun had a favorable impact in reducing turnover.
It was found that fun activities had a favorable impact on performance and manager support for fun had a favorable impact in reducing turnover.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "goals"。题干中的 "company's" 对应文章中的 "organizational"，"fit with" 对应 "aligned with"，"activities" 对应 "fun"。通过这些同义表达的对应关系，可以确定答案为 "goals"。题干要求填入一个词，"goals" 符合文章中的用词，并且与题干的意思完全一致。

central_sentences.sentence: Their findings support the view that fun may indeed have a beneficial effect, but the framing of that fun must be carefully aligned with both organizational goals and employee characteristics.
central_sentences.translation: 他们的研究结果支持这样的观点：趣味活动确实可能产生有益的效果，但这些趣味活动的框架必须要谨慎地与组织目标和员工特征保持一致。

target_sen.target_sen: Their findings support the view that fun may indeed have a beneficial effect, but the framing of that fun must be carefully aligned with both organizational goals and employee characteristics.
The impact of fun as a motivating factor at work has also been explored.
It was found that fun activities had a favorable impact on performance and manager support for fun had a favorable impact in reducing turnover.
Their findings support the view that fun may indeed have a beneficial effect, but the framing of that fun must be carefully aligned with both organizational goals and employee characteristics.
Their findings support the view that fun may indeed have a beneficial effect, but the framing of that fun must be carefully aligned with both organizational goals and employee characteristics.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“company's”和“staff”分别对应原文中的“organizational”和“employee”。在原文中，句子明确指出“the framing of that fun must be carefully aligned with both organizational goals and employee characteristics”，这与题干中的结构完全一致。因此，答案是“characteristics”。

central_sentences.sentence: Their findings support the view that fun may indeed have a beneficial effect, but the framing of that fun must be carefully aligned with both organizational goals and employee characteristics.
central_sentences.translation: 他们的研究结果支持这样一种观点：趣味活动确实可能产生有益的效果，但这种趣味活动的框架必须要仔细地与组织目标和员工特征相一致。

target_sen.target_sen: It was found that fun activities had a favorable impact on performance and manager support for fun had a favorable impact in reducing turnover.
Their findings support the view that fun may indeed have a beneficial effect, but the framing of that fun must be carefully aligned with both organizational goals and employee characteristics.
Their findings support the view that fun may indeed have a beneficial effect, but the framing of that fun must be carefully aligned with both organizational goals and employee characteristics.

'Managers must learn how to achieve the delicate balance of allowing employees the freedom to enjoy themselves at work while simultaneously maintaining high levels of performance' (Tews et al., 2013).
